content.problemStatement:
You are starting out on a long (really long) trip. On the way, there are N gas stations, the locations of which are given as a_1,a_2,...,a_N. Initially you are located at the gas station at a_1, and your destination is at location a_N. Your car can only store enough fuel to travel atmost M units without refilling. You can stop at any station and refill the car by any amount. Now you wish to plan your trip such that the number of intermediate stops needed to reach the destination is minimum, and also how many ways are there to plan your trip accordingly.



Input :

Output :
content.inputFormat: First line contains two integers N and M, where N is the number of gas stations and M is the maximum distance the car can travel without refilling. The next N lines contain the locations of the gas stations.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of intermediate stops needed to reach the destination and the number of ways to plan the trip accordingly.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The minimum number of stops needed to reach the last gas station is 3. The number of ways to plan the trip is 2.
The minimum number of stops needed to reach the last gas station is 2. The number of ways to plan the trip is 1.
The minimum number of stops needed to reach the last gas station is 1. The number of ways to plan the trip is 1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming with memoization. We create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the minimum number of stops needed to reach the jth gas station from the ith gas station. We also create another 2D array ways where ways[i][j] represents the number of ways to reach the jth gas station from the ith gas station. We fill up these arrays using a bottom-up approach and finally return the minimum number of stops and the number of ways to reach the last gas station from the first gas station.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) per test case, O(T*N^3) total. The time complexity is cubic due to the three nested loops.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the 2D array dp and ways.
